# slog

```go
import "github.com/go-coldbrew/log/loggers/slog"
```

Package slog provides a BaseLogger implementation for log/slog.

## Index

- [func NewLogger\(options ...loggers.Option\) loggers.BaseLogger](<#NewLogger>)
- [func NewLoggerWithHandler\(handler slog.Handler, options ...loggers.Option\) loggers.BaseLogger](<#NewLoggerWithHandler>)


<a name="NewLogger"></a>
## func [NewLogger](<https://github.com/go-coldbrew/log/blob/main/loggers/slog/slog.go#L152>)

```go
func NewLogger(options ...loggers.Option) loggers.BaseLogger
```

NewLogger returns a BaseLogger implementation backed by log/slog.

<a name="NewLoggerWithHandler"></a>
## func [NewLoggerWithHandler](<https://github.com/go-coldbrew/log/blob/main/loggers/slog/slog.go#L203>)

```go
func NewLoggerWithHandler(handler slog.Handler, options ...loggers.Option) loggers.BaseLogger
```

NewLoggerWithHandler returns a BaseLogger implementation backed by the provided slog.Handler. Use this when you need a custom handler \(e.g., for testing or custom output formats\). Note: SetLevel updates the internally tracked level but the provided handler's own level filtering takes precedence for actual output control.
